meta.name: Mexican Rice and Beans
meta.subtitle: Arroz rojo (tomato rice) with frijoles (simple stovetop beans) — a flexible weeknight staple

summary: A canonical SSA baseline for Mexican-style rice and beans: fluffy tomato rice (arroz rojo) plus simple seasoned beans. Supports canned-bean speed mode and dry-bean slow mode with the same flavor profile.

yield.notes: Serves ~6 as a side (rice + beans). Increase for large families or meal prep.

optional.label: Fine Strainer
optional.notes: Useful for rinsing rice and beans

ingredients.label: Long-grain white rice
ingredients.notes: Rinsed until water runs mostly clear

ingredients.label: Tomato sauce
ingredients.notes: Use instead of fresh tomatoes for speed

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Divide between rice + beans; adjust for broth saltiness

ingredients.label: Pinto beans, canned (drained and rinsed)
ingredients.notes: 15–16 oz cans; see variations for dry beans

ingredients.label: Water or broth (for beans)
ingredients.notes: Add as needed for desired sauciness

ingredients.label: Lime
ingredients.notes: Serve with wedges or squeeze into beans

steps.title: Prep aromatics and rinse rice
steps.body: Dice onion and mince garlic. Rinse rice in a fine strainer until water runs mostly clear; drain well. If using fresh tomatoes, blend them (or finely chop/juice them) to make a quick tomato puree.

steps.title: Toast the rice for fluffy texture
steps.body: In a saucepan over medium heat, warm 1 tbsp oil. Add rice and toast, stirring often, until lightly golden and nutty-smelling. Add half the onion and half the garlic; cook 1–2 minutes until fragrant (do not burn).

steps.title: Add tomato base and simmer rice
steps.body: Stir in tomato puree (or tomato sauce). Add broth, cumin and oregano (optional), and about 1 tsp salt (adjust for salty broth). Bring to a gentle boil, then cover and reduce to low. Simmer 18–20 minutes. Turn off heat and let rest covered 5–10 minutes, then fluff with a fork.

steps.title: Start quick beans
steps.body: In a pot over medium heat, warm 1 tbsp oil. Add remaining onion and sauté until soft. Add remaining garlic and cook 30 seconds. Stir in drained beans, bay leaf (optional), cumin (optional), and 1/2 tsp salt to start. Add 1/2–1 cup water or broth for sauciness.

steps.title: Simmer beans and adjust texture
steps.body: Simmer 8–12 minutes, stirring occasionally. For creamier beans, mash a small portion against the pot and stir back in. Adjust salt and pepper. Remove bay leaf before serving.

steps.title: Finish and serve
steps.body: Serve rice and beans together. Optional: squeeze lime over beans, garnish with cilantro. Great alongside tacos, enchiladas, grilled meats, or vegetables.

variations.label: Vegetarian / Vegan
variations.notes: Use vegetable broth for rice and beans. Finish with lime and cilantro for brightness. Add sautéed peppers for extra body.

variations.label: Refried-Style Beans (Quick)
variations.notes: Simmer beans with minimal liquid, then mash 1/2–2/3 of the beans. Cook a few minutes more until thick and creamy.

variations.label: From Dry Pinto Beans
variations.notes: Soak 1 lb dry pinto beans overnight (optional). Simmer with onion, garlic, bay leaf, and salt until tender (60–120 min). Then season and thicken as desired. This increases total time significantly.

variations.label: Rice Cooker Method
variations.notes: Toast rice/aromatics in a pan first if possible, then transfer to rice cooker with tomato base + broth. Cook using white rice setting; rest 10 minutes and fluff.

variations.label: Spicy
variations.notes: Add diced jalapeño or a pinch of cayenne to beans. Add chipotle in adobo for smoky heat.

storage.refrigeration: Refrigerate rice and beans separately (best texture) for up to 4 days.
storage.freezing: Freeze beans for up to 3 months. Rice can be frozen, but texture is best fresh; if freezing rice, cool quickly and reheat with a splash of water.
storage.reheat: Reheat rice covered with a splash of water; reheat beans with a splash of broth/water until steaming hot.

notes: Cool cooked rice and beans within 2 hours before refrigerating.
Reheat leftovers to steaming hot throughout.

planner.leftoversPlan: Pack rice and beans in separate compartments/containers to keep rice fluffy; include lime wedge and salsa cup.

notes: Canonical SSA baseline supports both quick canned-bean mode and slower dry-bean mode via variations.
To avoid mushy rice: toast well, keep lid closed during simmer, and rest before fluffing.
Salt levels depend heavily on broth brand; season at the end for best control.
